question: Will **real GDP** increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: Based on the latest nowcast models, the probability of Q1 2026 real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% appears significantly lower than the market price suggests. The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model has revised its Q1 2026 estimate down to 1.6% as of early April, falling from over 2% in late March due to softer retail sales and personal consumption data. While the New York Fed's nowcast is higher at 2.4%, the conflicting signals and the fact that the most closely-watched Atlanta Fed model is well below the 2.0% threshold suggests the true probability is around 30%, creating substantial negative edge against the 54% market price.
key_factors: <cite index="11-9,17-2">Atlanta Fed GDPNow model at 1.6% as of early April 2026</cite>
<cite index="17-2">New York Fed nowcast higher at 2.4%</cite>
<cite index="17-2">Softer retail sales and personal consumption outlays data driving downward revisions</cite>
<cite index="9-3,9-4">Q4 2025 GDP growth was only 0.7% (second estimate)</cite>
<cite index="1-8,1-9">Atlanta Fed GDPNow has historical accuracy with 0.77 percentage point average absolute error</cite>
risks: New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% suggests upside potential
GDP data subject to revisions and final estimates may differ from advance estimates
Strong labor market data (unemployment at 4.3%) could support higher growth
Geopolitical tensions and oil price impacts creating economic uncertainty
Only 22 days until April 30 BEA advance estimate release
